Job Position: General Manager

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	As the General Manager, you will oversee company-wide operations, sales, and strategic direction. You will drive business performance, ensure operational excellence, and lead cross-functional teams to achieve organizational growth and profitability.


Responsibilities


	Lead strategic planning and business development initiatives to achieve company goals
	Oversee sales, operations, and administrative functions to ensure efficiency and alignment
	Manage budgets, financial performance, and resource allocation across business units
	Develop and implement policies, performance frameworks, and company-wide objectives
	Foster strong relationships with stakeholders and provide leadership to cross-functional teams


Requirements


	B.Sc. in Business Administration, Management, or related field (Master&rsquo;s degree preferred)
	Minimum of 8 years of leadership experience in sales, operations, or general management
	Proven track record in business growth and performance management
	Strong leadership, communication, and decision-making skills
	Financially literate with experience in budget planning and cost control.


Salary
NGN 800,000 - NGN 1,000,000 / Month.",

General Manager Job at TeamAce Limited




        
    

        
        TeamAce Limited: Our client, a reputable company in the elevator and lifting solutions sector, is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: General Manager

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	As the General Manager, you will oversee company-wide operations, sales, and strategic direction. You will drive business performance, ensure operational excellence, and lead cross-functional teams to achieve organizational growth and profitability.


Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Lead strategic planning and business development initiatives to achieve company goals
	Oversee sales, operations, and administrative functions to ensure efficiency and alignment
	Manage budgets, financial performance, and resource allocation across business units
	Develop and implement policies, performance frameworks, and company-wide objectives
	Foster strong relationships with stakeholders and provide leadership to cross-functional teams


Requirements


	B.Sc. in Business Administration, Management, or related field (Master’s degree preferred)
	Minimum of 8 years of leadership experience in sales, operations, or general management
	Proven track record in business growth and performance management
	Strong leadership, communication, and decision-making skills
	Financially literate with experience in budget planning and cost control.


Salary
NGN 800,000 - NGN 1,000,000 / Month.    

    

        Click here to Apply

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Manager, Strategy (Public Sector)

Role Overview&nbsp;

	Lead and manage public sector consulting engagements from planning through execution.&nbsp;

What We&rsquo;re Looking For:

	Minimum of 6 years&rsquo; experience in government, public sector advisory, or consulting (strategy or boutique firms), with exposure to public sector reforms and transformation initiatives; sector experience is a plus.&nbsp;
	Strong proficiency in written and spoken English.&nbsp;
	Demonstrated ability to structure and decompose complex public sector challenges into clear, actionable solutions.&nbsp;
	Experience preparing high-quality consulting-style deliverables, including policy briefs, presentations, status reports, meeting notes, and project tracking documents.&nbsp;
	Excellent stakeholder management and relationship-building skills, with the ability to engage effectively with government officials, development partners, and cross-functional teams.&nbsp;
	Strong leadership and facilitation skills, particularly in multi-stakeholder environments.&nbsp;
	Highly developed analytical, problem-solving, and adaptability skills.&nbsp;
	Excellent communication, presentation, and policy storytelling abilities.&nbsp;
	Full computer literacy, including proficiency in standard business and analytical tools.&nbsp;

Manager, Strategy (Public Sector) Job at PricewaterhouseCoopers (PwC) Nigeria




        
    

        
        PricewaterhouseCoopers (PwC) Nigeria has been operating in Nigeria since 1953 through its predecessor firms of Coopers & Lybrand and Price Waterhouse. We're one of the leading professional services firms in the country with offices in Lagos, Abuja and Port Harcourt, over 1000 staff and 31 resident partners.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Manager, Strategy (Public Sector)

Job requisition ID: 737080WD
Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full time
Management Level: Manager

Role Overview 
     

            
    




	We are looking for a results-driven Manager to join our team. This role involves leading projects that support government and public institutions in achieving policy, operational, and digital transformation goals.
	The ideal candidate will have a strong understanding of public sector dynamics and a passion for driving impact through strategic advisory and implementation. 


Key Responsibilities 


	Lead and manage public sector consulting engagements from planning through execution. 
	Provide strategic advice to government clients on policy, governance, and operational improvements. 
	Design and implement public sector reform and digital transformation initiatives. 
	Build and maintain strong relationships with public sector stakeholders. 
	Support business development efforts including proposal writing and client presentations. 
	Guide and mentor junior consultants and analysts. 


Requirements
What We’re Looking For:


	A First Degree is required; an MBA, MSc., or PhD in a relevant field such as Public Policy, Economics, Finance, or Business Administration is an added advantage. 
	Minimum of 6 years’ experience in government, public sector advisory, or consulting (strategy or boutique firms), with exposure to public sector reforms and transformation initiatives; sector experience is a plus. 
	Strong proficiency in written and spoken English. 
	Demonstrated ability to structure and decompose complex public sector challenges into clear, actionable solutions. 
	Experience preparing high-quality consulting-style deliverables, including policy briefs, presentations, status reports, meeting notes, and project tracking documents. 
	Excellent stakeholder management and relationship-building skills, with the ability to engage effectively with government officials, development partners, and cross-functional teams. 
	Strong leadership and facilitation skills, particularly in multi-stakeholder environments. 
	Highly developed analytical, problem-solving, and adaptability skills. 
	Excellent communication, presentation, and policy storytelling abilities. 
	Full computer literacy, including proficiency in standard business and analytical tools. 
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Head of Product Management",
  "description": "Union Bank of Nigeria (&quot;UBN&quot;) was established in 1917 and is one of Nigeria&rsquo;s long-standing and most respected financial institutions, offering a portfolio of banking services to individuals, SMEs, commercial and corporate clients. With a robust geographical network comprising more than 300 service centres and over 950+ ATMs spread across Nigeria, we have remained committed to helping individuals, families and businesses grow for nearly a century.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Product Management

Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full time

Role Description&nbsp;


	The Head of Product Management is a full-time, hybrid role based in Lagos, with flexibility for some work-from-home arrangements.&nbsp;
	This role leads the end-to-end product strategy and lifecycle management across Union Bank&rsquo;s portfolio, ensuring products are customer-centric, commercially viable, and aligned with the bank&rsquo;s digital and business objectives.&nbsp;
	Day-to-day responsibilities include defining product vision, prioritizing product roadmaps, overseeing product development and enhancements, and coordinating cross-functional teams such as technology, operations, marketing, and sales.&nbsp;
	The Head of Product Management will analyze market trends, customer insights, and performance data to optimize existing products and identify new opportunities.&nbsp;
	The role also involves mentoring product managers, establishing best practices, and ensuring compliance with regulatory and risk management standards.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business, Finance, Engineering, or a related field; a relevant Master&rsquo;s Degree or MBA is an advantage.
	Experience in banking, financial services, or fintech, with knowledge of digital channels and regulatory requirements.
	Demonstrated expertise in Product Strategy and Product Management, with a track record of leading successful product portfolios.
	Strong experience in Product Lifecycle Management, including ideation, development, launch, and continuous improvement.
	Advanced Analytical Skills, with the ability to interpret data, market research, and customer insights to inform decisions.
	Background collaborating with Sales teams to drive product adoption, revenue growth, and customer engagement.
	Proven leadership experience managing product teams, setting priorities, and driving cross-functional alignment.
	Excellent communication, stakeholder management, and presentation skills suitable for senior leadership settings.
	Ability to work effectively in a hybrid environment, demonstrating flexibility, accountability, and strong time management.

Head of Product Management Job at Union Bank of Nigeria




        
    

        
        Union Bank of Nigeria ("UBN") was established in 1917 and is one of Nigeria’s long-standing and most respected financial institutions, offering a portfolio of banking services to individuals, SMEs, commercial and corporate clients. With a robust geographical network comprising more than 300 service centres and over 950+ ATMs spread across Nigeria, we have remained committed to helping individuals, families and businesses grow for nearly a century.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Product Management

Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full time

Role Description 
     

            
    




	The Head of Product Management is a full-time, hybrid role based in Lagos, with flexibility for some work-from-home arrangements. 
	This role leads the end-to-end product strategy and lifecycle management across Union Bank’s portfolio, ensuring products are customer-centric, commercially viable, and aligned with the bank’s digital and business objectives. 
	Day-to-day responsibilities include defining product vision, prioritizing product roadmaps, overseeing product development and enhancements, and coordinating cross-functional teams such as technology, operations, marketing, and sales. 
	The Head of Product Management will analyze market trends, customer insights, and performance data to optimize existing products and identify new opportunities. 
	The role also involves mentoring product managers, establishing best practices, and ensuring compliance with regulatory and risk management standards.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business, Finance, Engineering, or a related field; a relevant Master’s Degree or MBA is an advantage.
	Experience in banking, financial services, or fintech, with knowledge of digital channels and regulatory requirements.
	Demonstrated expertise in Product Strategy and Product Management, with a track record of leading successful product portfolios.
	Strong experience in Product Lifecycle Management, including ideation, development, launch, and continuous improvement.
	Advanced Analytical Skills, with the ability to interpret data, market research, and customer insights to inform decisions.
	Background collaborating with Sales teams to drive product adoption, revenue growth, and customer engagement.
	Proven leadership experience managing product teams, setting priorities, and driving cross-functional alignment.
	Excellent communication, stakeholder management, and presentation skills suitable for senior leadership settings.
	Ability to work effectively in a hybrid environment, demonstrating flexibility, accountability, and strong time management.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Administrative & Student Support Officer",
  "description": "We provide exceptional HR support that empowers businesses to succeed. We&#39;re committed to delivering tailored solutions that meet the unique needs of each client, from recruitment and talent management to compliance and risk management.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative &amp; Student Support Officer
Job Location: Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Administrative &amp; Student Support Officer will provide administrative and operational support to the Institute while serving as theliaison between prospective students, enrolled students, faculty, and Head Office.
	The successful candidate will manage student communications across multiple platforms, coordinate administrative processes, support programme delivery, and ensure a seamless student experience from enquiry through course completion.


Key Responsibilities


	Serve as apoint of contact for prospective and enrolled students across all communication channels.
	Respond promptly and professionally to enquiries received via Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, WhatsApp, email, website chat, and other digital platforms.
	Guide prospective students through the admissions and enrolment process by providing accurate and timely information.
	Maintain regular communication with enrolled students regarding classes, schedules, assessments, examinations, and institute announcements.
	Coordinate communication between Head Office, faculty, facilitators, and students to ensure seamless programme delivery.
	Maintain accurate student records, enrolment data, attendance registers, and administrative documentation.
	Coordinate class schedules, learning materials, training logistics, and programme administration.
	Support the planning and execution of training sessions, workshops, examinations, graduation ceremonies, and other institute activities.
	Monitor and follow up on student enquiries, registrations, documentation, and outstanding administrative requirements.
	Support the implementation of operational processes and ensure compliance with the Institute&#39;s policies and procedures.
	Prepare reports, correspondence, presentations, certificates, and other administrative documents as required.
	Escalate student concerns and operational issues to the appropriate personnel for timely resolution.
	Provide general administrative support and perform other duties assigned by Management.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Business Administration, Office Administration, Education, Management, Mass Communication, or a related discipline.
	Minimum of 2 years&#39; experience in an administrative, student support, customer service, or operations role.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills,strong customer service, interpersonal, and relationship management skills.
	Experience managing enquiries across social media platforms, WhatsApp Business, email, and other digital communication channels.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and Google Workspace.
	Strong organizational, planning, and multitasking abilities with excellent attention to detail.
	Ability to maintain accurate records and manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
	Professional, proactive, and able to maintain confidentiality at all times.
	Experience working in an educational institution, training institute, or professional services environment will be an added advantage.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 Monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV and portfolio to: peopleplusng@gmail.com using &quot;Administrative &amp; Student Support Officer &ndash; Abuja&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Administrative & Student Support Officer Job at  Peopleplusng




        
    

        
        We provide exceptional HR support that empowers businesses to succeed. We're committed to delivering tailored solutions that meet the unique needs of each client, from recruitment and talent management to compliance and risk management.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative & Student Support Officer
Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The Administrative & Student Support Officer will provide administrative and operational support to the Institute while serving as theliaison between prospective students, enrolled students, faculty, and Head Office.
	The successful candidate will manage student communications across multiple platforms, coordinate administrative processes, support programme delivery, and ensure a seamless student experience from enquiry through course completion.


Key Responsibilities


	Serve as apoint of contact for prospective and enrolled students across all communication channels.
	Respond promptly and professionally to enquiries received via Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, WhatsApp, email, website chat, and other digital platforms.
	Guide prospective students through the admissions and enrolment process by providing accurate and timely information.
	Maintain regular communication with enrolled students regarding classes, schedules, assessments, examinations, and institute announcements.
	Coordinate communication between Head Office, faculty, facilitators, and students to ensure seamless programme delivery.
	Maintain accurate student records, enrolment data, attendance registers, and administrative documentation.
	Coordinate class schedules, learning materials, training logistics, and programme administration.
	Support the planning and execution of training sessions, workshops, examinations, graduation ceremonies, and other institute activities.
	Monitor and follow up on student enquiries, registrations, documentation, and outstanding administrative requirements.
	Support the implementation of operational processes and ensure compliance with the Institute's policies and procedures.
	Prepare reports, correspondence, presentations, certificates, and other administrative documents as required.
	Escalate student concerns and operational issues to the appropriate personnel for timely resolution.
	Provide general administrative support and perform other duties assigned by Management.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Business Administration, Office Administration, Education, Management, Mass Communication, or a related discipline.
	Minimum of 2 years' experience in an administrative, student support, customer service, or operations role.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills,strong customer service, interpersonal, and relationship management skills.
	Experience managing enquiries across social media platforms, WhatsApp Business, email, and other digital communication channels.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and Google Workspace.
	Strong organizational, planning, and multitasking abilities with excellent attention to detail.
	Ability to maintain accurate records and manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
	Professional, proactive, and able to maintain confidentiality at all times.
	Experience working in an educational institution, training institute, or professional services environment will be an added advantage.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 Monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV and portfolio to: [email protected] using "Administrative & Student Support Officer – Abuja" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Head of Technology",
  "description": "AISHA (AI supported Healthcare Assistant) is an advanced AI healthcare assistant designed to deliver personalized, accessible, and intelligent healthcare experiences to users across Africa and beyond. AISHA leverages cutting-edge natural language processing, clinical decision support, and machine learning to provide real-time health guidance, symptom assessment, medication management, and care coordination &mdash; bridging the gap between patients and quality healthcare services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Technology

Job Location: Lagos

Role Summary


	The Head of Technology is a senior leadership role responsible for driving the full technology vision, architecture, and execution of the AISHA platform.
	This individual will lead a cross-functional engineering and AI team, oversee the design and scalability of the platform, ensure regulatory and data compliance, and translate product vision into robust, secure, and high-performing technical systems.
	The ideal candidate combines deep technical expertise in AI/ML and health technology with strong people leadership and a passion for impact-driven innovation.


Key Responsibilities
Technology Strategy &amp; Vision:


	Define and execute AISHA&#39;s technology roadmap in alignment with product and business objectives.
	Lead architectural decisions across the AI, backend, frontend, and data layers of the platform.
	Stay ahead of emerging technologies in AI, health tech, and conversational agents, integrating relevant advances into AISHA.
	Advise executive leadership on technology investments, infrastructure, and capability building.


AI &amp; Product Development:


	Oversee development and continuous improvement of AISHA&#39;s core AI models &mdash; including NLP, symptom checker, diagnostic reasoning, and health recommendation engines.
	Collaborate closely with the Product team to translate user needs and clinical requirements into technical specifications.
	Drive LLM integration, fine-tuning, and responsible AI deployment practices.
	Ensure AI outputs are clinically appropriate, bias-mitigated, and explainable.


Engineering Leadership:


	Recruit, manage, and mentor a high-performing team of engineers, AI/ML researchers, and data scientists.
	Establish engineering best practices including agile delivery, code review, CI/CD pipelines, and documentation standards.
	Foster a culture of innovation, ownership, and technical excellence within the team.
	Manage vendor relationships and third-party integrations (e.g., EHR systems, telehealth APIs, payment gateways).


Infrastructure, Security &amp; Compliance:


	Own the platform&#39;s infrastructure, ensuring high availability, scalability, and performance.
	Ensure AISHA meets all applicable data privacy regulations including NDPR (Nigeria Data Protection Regulation), HIPAA principles, and relevant health data governance frameworks.
	Lead cybersecurity strategy, risk management, and incident response protocols.
	Oversee cloud infrastructure (AWS/GCP/Azure) and ensure cost-effective resource management.


Stakeholder &amp; Cross-Functional Collaboration:


	Work closely with clinical advisors, product managers, and business leads to ensure technology serves health outcomes.
	Represent technology in board presentations, investor meetings, and partner discussions.
	Collaborate with the Regulatory and Legal team to ensure platform compliance with Nigerian and international health tech standards.


Qualifications &amp; Requirements
Education:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Electrical/Electronic Engineering, or a related field. (Required)
	Master&rsquo;s degree in Artificial Intelligence, Health Informatics, or an MBA with a technology focus. (Preferred)


Experience:


	Minimum of 8 years of progressive experience in software/product engineering, with at least 3 years in a senior technical leadership role.
	Demonstrated experience building and scaling AI-powered or data-driven products &mdash; healthtech experience is a strong advantage.
	Proven track record of leading end-to-end product technology delivery from MVP to growth stage.
	Experience working in or with African markets; understanding of low-bandwidth and mobile-first user environments is a plus.


Technical Skills:


	Strong proficiency in Python, and/or JavaScript/TypeScript; familiarity with Go or Rust is a plus.
	Deep expertise in machine learning frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch) and LLM orchestration tools (LangChain, OpenAI API, Anthropic API, etc.).
	Proficient in cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure), containerization (Docker/Kubernetes), and micro services architecture.
	Experience with FHIR, HL7, or other health data interoperability standards is an advantage.
	Familiarity with vector databases, RAG pipelines, and AI agent frameworks.


Leadership &amp; Soft Skills:


	Exceptional leadership and team-building skills with the ability to inspire and manage diverse technical teams.
	Strong strategic thinking balanced with hands-on technical credibility.
	Excellent communication skills &mdash; ability to translate complex technical concepts for non-technical stakeholders.
	High sense of ownership, accountability, and mission alignment with improving healthcare access.


Benefits


	Competitive compensation and equity participation.
	Opportunity to build and lead technology at the intersection of AI and healthcare in one of Africa&rsquo;s fastest-growing markets.
	Flexible hybrid work arrangement.
	Access to continuous learning resources, AI research communities, and industry conferences.
	A mission-driven team passionate about expanding access to quality healthcare through technology.

Head of Technology Job at AISHA (AI supported Healthcare Assistant)




        
    

        
        AISHA (AI supported Healthcare Assistant) is an advanced AI healthcare assistant designed to deliver personalized, accessible, and intelligent healthcare experiences to users across Africa and beyond. AISHA leverages cutting-edge natural language processing, clinical decision support, and machine learning to provide real-time health guidance, symptom assessment, medication management, and care coordination — bridging the gap between patients and quality healthcare services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head of Technology

Job Location: Lagos

Role Summary
     

            
    




	The Head of Technology is a senior leadership role responsible for driving the full technology vision, architecture, and execution of the AISHA platform.
	This individual will lead a cross-functional engineering and AI team, oversee the design and scalability of the platform, ensure regulatory and data compliance, and translate product vision into robust, secure, and high-performing technical systems.
	The ideal candidate combines deep technical expertise in AI/ML and health technology with strong people leadership and a passion for impact-driven innovation.


Key Responsibilities
Technology Strategy & Vision:


	Define and execute AISHA's technology roadmap in alignment with product and business objectives.
	Lead architectural decisions across the AI, backend, frontend, and data layers of the platform.
	Stay ahead of emerging technologies in AI, health tech, and conversational agents, integrating relevant advances into AISHA.
	Advise executive leadership on technology investments, infrastructure, and capability building.


AI & Product Development:


	Oversee development and continuous improvement of AISHA's core AI models — including NLP, symptom checker, diagnostic reasoning, and health recommendation engines.
	Collaborate closely with the Product team to translate user needs and clinical requirements into technical specifications.
	Drive LLM integration, fine-tuning, and responsible AI deployment practices.
	Ensure AI outputs are clinically appropriate, bias-mitigated, and explainable.


Engineering Leadership:


	Recruit, manage, and mentor a high-performing team of engineers, AI/ML researchers, and data scientists.
	Establish engineering best practices including agile delivery, code review, CI/CD pipelines, and documentation standards.
	Foster a culture of innovation, ownership, and technical excellence within the team.
	Manage vendor relationships and third-party integrations (e.g., EHR systems, telehealth APIs, payment gateways).


Infrastructure, Security & Compliance:


	Own the platform's infrastructure, ensuring high availability, scalability, and performance.
	Ensure AISHA meets all applicable data privacy regulations including NDPR (Nigeria Data Protection Regulation), HIPAA principles, and relevant health data governance frameworks.
	Lead cybersecurity strategy, risk management, and incident response protocols.
	Oversee cloud infrastructure (AWS/GCP/Azure) and ensure cost-effective resource management.


Stakeholder & Cross-Functional Collaboration:


	Work closely with clinical advisors, product managers, and business leads to ensure technology serves health outcomes.
	Represent technology in board presentations, investor meetings, and partner discussions.
	Collaborate with the Regulatory and Legal team to ensure platform compliance with Nigerian and international health tech standards.


Qualifications & Requirements
Education:


	Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Electrical/Electronic Engineering, or a related field. (Required)
	Master’s degree in Artificial Intelligence, Health Informatics, or an MBA with a technology focus. (Preferred)


Experience:


	Minimum of 8 years of progressive experience in software/product engineering, with at least 3 years in a senior technical leadership role.
	Demonstrated experience building and scaling AI-powered or data-driven products — healthtech experience is a strong advantage.
	Proven track record of leading end-to-end product technology delivery from MVP to growth stage.
	Experience working in or with African markets; understanding of low-bandwidth and mobile-first user environments is a plus.


Technical Skills:


	Strong proficiency in Python, and/or JavaScript/TypeScript; familiarity with Go or Rust is a plus.
	Deep expertise in machine learning frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch) and LLM orchestration tools (LangChain, OpenAI API, Anthropic API, etc.).
	Proficient in cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure), containerization (Docker/Kubernetes), and micro services architecture.
	Experience with FHIR, HL7, or other health data interoperability standards is an advantage.
	Familiarity with vector databases, RAG pipelines, and AI agent frameworks.


Leadership & Soft Skills:


	Exceptional leadership and team-building skills with the ability to inspire and manage diverse technical teams.
	Strong strategic thinking balanced with hands-on technical credibility.
	Excellent communication skills — ability to translate complex technical concepts for non-technical stakeholders.
	High sense of ownership, accountability, and mission alignment with improving healthcare access.


Benefits


	Competitive compensation and equity participation.
	Opportunity to build and lead technology at the intersection of AI and healthcare in one of Africa’s fastest-growing markets.
	Flexible hybrid work arrangement.
	Access to continuous learning resources, AI research communities, and industry conferences.
	A mission-driven team passionate about expanding access to quality healthcare through technology.
